author: jtraglia
content: Hi all. Creating this thread to discuss <@881905303011086387>'s PR: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5152

author: jtraglia
content: Etan does this affect the issue we were chatting about with `ExecutionPayloadHeader`?

author: jtraglia
content: Regardless, if this makes it easier for light clients, this PR seems like a reasonable ask to me.

author: jtraglia
content: I'd like to hear what <@755590043632140352> thinks too.

author: etan_status
content: no, latest_execution_block_payload -> latest_block_hash is orthogonal. that one just increases the cost for obtaining basic metadata like block number or gas usage, as one now has to go via JSON-RPC (often another paid API call) or extra latency in a portal style world. but it is not preventing designs, just making the happy case more expensive.

author: etan_status
content: this one is solely about retaining the ability to sync an EL with newPayload + fcU using just a single gossip topic for each of the purposes

author: etan_status
content: newPayload from envelopes topic, and fcU from light client topic

author: etan_status
content: originally, parent_beacon_block_root was in the payload. we removed it because the surrounding beaconblock already contains it, and we didn't want to duplicate it

author: etan_status
content: but now the payload was moved, so we just have to move the parent_beacon_block_root to the new surrounding container (i.e., the envelope, now)

author: etan_status
content: engine_newPayload needs 4 things: the payload itself, the blob versioned hashes / kzg commitments (derivable from payload.transactions), the requests (part of the envelope), and the parent beacon block root (currently unavailable when just following envelopes gossip)
